sn38.16:1.1: "Friend Sāriputta, what is difficult to do in this Dhamma and Discipline?"
sn38.16:1.2: "Going forth, friend, is difficult to do in this Dhamma and Discipline."
sn38.16:1.3: "What, friend, is difficult to do by one who has gone forth?"
sn38.16:1.4: "To find delight, friend, is difficult to do by one who has gone forth."
sn38.16:1.5: "What, friend, is difficult to do by one who has found delight?"
sn38.16:1.6: "Practice in accordance with the Dhamma, friend, is difficult to do by one who has found delight."
sn38.16:1.7: "But, friend, if a bhikkhu is practising in accordance with the Dhamma, would it take him long to become an arahant?"
sn38.16:1.8: "Not long, friend."[n.275] Spk quotes MN II 96,19–20: "Instructed in the evening, by the morning he will attain distinction (enlightenment); instructed in the morning, by the evening he will attain distinction."
